I'm so glad I didn't watch the trailer before seeing the movie. Even without the full context, trailers really do be spoiling every moment that would hit much harder without already knowing it when going in, like bruh, just let me be surprised movie making people.

@tompeng8248 I already saw it and I loved it, the characters were interesting, and SPOILER ALERT I love how Ceasar after generations after his passing has become this legendary figure, and his ideas and values become misconstrued into becoming the very thing he fought against. Awesome movie, and honestly was the only movie in theaters that i was excited for.

@tompeng8248 Yeah okay dude, judge my taste in movies and equate it to intelligence level. I'm sure you have all the superior insight on everything that's good and what isn't. 🙄 didn't even have the reading comprehension to tell that I've already seen the movie. Does make me curious what YOU consider to be worth watching though, I'm sure I'll think you have poor taste as well. 👌

@tompeng8248 Nah bro, don't try to act like you said one thing when you clearly didn't, your first comment started with, "don't watch it, save your money," when it should've been clear from my initial comment that I already went to see it. It was only after I clarified that I already saw it that you said, "I thought you were smarter," sheesh.

Also for your taste in movies, congratulations, I can't say it's complete trash as I haven't even seen, "where the crawdads sing" or the latest hunger games, although I can say M3GAN is pretty up there in being a very basic and goofy, and just overall pretty mediocre and forgetful movie. You even got some solid movie choices for the older ones, I like Apocalypto, and Silence of the Lambs is an obvious classic, but Signs? Really? That one is pretty meh, Shyamalan really started showing some lesser effort with this one, and the "twist" was dumb as all hell. 

What I think I'm having a tough time understanding is where your hate for the new Planet of the Apes films is coming from. The characters are very easy to sympathize with and relate to, their struggles feel real, and each movie takes the series in interesting directions that even make subtle references to the original and admittedly pretty goofy older movies that they're expanding on. I almost want to assume that you haven't even seen any of them, and just think they're dumb without even giving them a watch and forming an actual opinion. Do the apes movies have flaws, sure, no movie is the end all be all perfect movie, but the stories they're telling are very topical and interesting, especially when thinking deeper about human behavior in relation to the newfound intelligence of the apes, and how they navigate that as the prior dominant species i.e. humans attempt to maintain their role at the top all while they've been quickly dying out or are at risk to becoming the lesser intelligence. 

All I'm saying is, your hate for it seems unfounded and based on literally nothing.
This movie tells us how human control this whole planet with negative ways.. reflected our treatment to other species
